title: Phase 12 — Angular Routing, HTTP, RxJS & Security

questions.question: Which module historically provided Router (still imported in many apps)?
questions.explanation: RouterModule.forRoot registers root routes and location strategy.

questions.question: What does `{ path: '**', component: NotFoundComponent }` typically do?
questions.explanation: ** is a wildcard route; order matters—usually last.

questions.question: How do you navigate programmatically?
questions.explanation: Router service performs in-app navigation.

questions.question: Route: `{ path: 'users/:id', component: UserComponent }`. URL `/users/42`. How to read id?
questions.explanation: paramMap exposes route parameters like id.

questions.question: What is lazy loading a feature module?
options: Loading module JS chunk on demand when route activates
questions.explanation: loadChildren triggers dynamic import of feature module.

questions.question: What does `declarations` array in @NgModule contain?
questions.explanation: declarations register components/directives/pipes owned by the module.

questions.question: What is the difference between `imports` and `providers` in NgModule?
options: imports: other modules; providers: DI tokens for this module scope
questions.explanation: imports compose module functionality; providers register services.

questions.question: In standalone components, what replaces NgModule `imports`?
options: Component imports: [] array of other standalone/directives/modules
questions.explanation: Standalone components list dependencies in their imports.

questions.question: What does HttpClient return by default for get<T>()?
questions.explanation: HttpClient methods return Observables.

questions.question: How do you send JSON body with POST?
questions.explanation: HttpClient serializes plain objects to JSON with appropriate headers.

questions.question: What does `observe: 'response'` option return?
options: Full HttpResponse including headers and status
questions.explanation: observe response wraps body with status/headers.

questions.question: Where are HTTP interceptors registered?
questions.explanation: Multi-provider HTTP_INTERCEPTORS chain requests/responses.

questions.question: What does Observable represent?
options: A lazy stream of values over time
questions.explanation: Observables push multiple values asynchronously.

questions.question: What does `subscribe()` do?
options: Starts execution and listens for emissions/errors/complete
questions.explanation: Subscription begins producer side effects for cold observables.

questions.question: What does pipe(map(x => x * 2)) do?
questions.explanation: map applies a projection function per emission.

questions.question: When should you use `switchMap` vs `mergeMap`?
options: switchMap cancels prior inner observable; mergeMap subscribes to all
questions.explanation: switchMap is ideal for search/autocomplete; mergeMap for parallel ops.

questions.question: What does `async` pipe do in templates?
options: Subscribes to Observable/Promise and marks view for check
questions.explanation: async pipe manages subscribe/unsubscribe in templates.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `takeUntil(destroy$)`?
options: Complete subscription when destroy$ emits (avoid leaks)
questions.explanation: Pattern ties stream lifetime to component destruction.

questions.question: What is a typical responsibility of an auth interceptor?
options: Attach Authorization: Bearer <token> header to outgoing requests
questions.explanation: Interceptors mutate requests/responses in the chain.

questions.question: Where is a JWT usually stored in SPA debates—common secure-ish pattern?
options: localStorage (XSS risk) vs httpOnly cookie (CSRF considerations)
questions.explanation: Storage choice trades XSS vs CSRF; httpOnly cookies need CSRF protection.

questions.question: On 401 from API, an interceptor might?
options: Trigger logout or token refresh flow
questions.explanation: Global handling can refresh token or redirect to login.

questions.question: What does `canActivate` guard control?
options: Whether a route may be activated
questions.explanation: canActivate returns boolean/UrlTree/Observable to allow navigation.

questions.question: What does `canDeactivate` guard protect?
options: Leaving a route with unsaved changes
questions.explanation: canDeactivate prompts before navigating away from a component.

questions.question: A guard returning `UrlTree` does what?
questions.explanation: UrlTree redirect is built-in router behavior.

questions.question: What is `routerLinkActive` used for?
options: Apply CSS class when link's route is active
questions.explanation: routerLinkActive toggles classes for active route links.

questions.question: Child routes are configured how?
questions.explanation: Nested routes render in parent router-outlet or named outlets.

questions.question: How to pass query params?
questions.explanation: HttpParams builds encoded query strings safely.

questions.question: What does `of(1,2,3)` create?
options: Synchronous cold Observable emitting those values then complete
questions.explanation: of emits arguments synchronously then completes.

questions.question: Difference between Subject and BehaviorSubject?
options: Subject stores last value for late subscribers
BehaviorSubject requires initial value and replays latest to new subscribers
questions.explanation: BehaviorSubject holds current value for new subscribers.

questions.question: What does `exports` in NgModule do?
options: Makes declared/imported artifacts available to importing modules
questions.explanation: exports expose components/directives/pipes to other modules.

questions.question: Error handling pattern for failed HTTP?
options: try/catch around subscribe only works for sync
questions.explanation: catchError operator transforms errors in Observable chain.

questions.question: JWT payload is typically?
options: Base64url-encoded JSON claims (signed, not encrypted by default)
questions.explanation: JWTs are signed; payload is readable unless JWE used.

questions.question: `canMatch` guard (modern) differs from `canActivate` by?
options: canMatch decides if route config matches before loading (e.g., role-based routes)
questions.explanation: canMatch filters which route config applies.

questions.question: Named router outlet syntax?
options: routerLink with outlet name in array or routerLinkActive
questions.explanation: Auxiliary routes use (outletName) in link arrays.

questions.question: What does `debounceTime(300)` help with?
options: Wait for pause in emissions (e.g., search input)
questions.explanation: debounceTime delays emissions until silence window.

questions.question: Testing HttpClient in Angular commonly uses?
questions.explanation: HttpTestingController expects and flushes mock requests.

questions.question: PreloadingStrategy purpose?
options: Load lazy modules in background after initial navigation
questions.explanation: Preload improves perceived performance for lazy routes.

questions.question: Unsubscribing is important because?
options: Leaked subscriptions keep handlers alive and may cause duplicate work
questions.explanation: Active subscriptions retain references and side effects.

questions.question: Functional guard `canActivate: [authGuard]` where authGuard is?
options: CanActivateFn injected via inject() returning boolean/UrlTree
questions.explanation: Modern functional guards use CanActivateFn.

questions.question: CSRF token pattern when using cookies for auth?
options: Send custom header/token that malicious sites cannot read cross-origin
questions.explanation: Double-submit cookie or custom header mitigates CSRF with cookies.

questions.question: SharedModule anti-pattern to avoid?
options: Re-exporting CommonModule + many components creating huge import graph
questions.explanation: Kitchen-sink SharedModule causes unnecessary bundle bloat.

questions.question: `reportProgress: true` enables?
questions.explanation: Progress events useful for large file uploads.
